text.content:
Phase 9 Pressure System

Phase 9 runs on three clocks. The party feels pressure from three different directions:

  • The Under-Road Alert Clock — how aware the waystation is of the party as a problem.
  • The Record Destruction Clock — how much evidence is being burned, scraped, moved, or corrupted ahead of the party.
  • The Breath and Stone Clock — how much environmental damage the party (or the route) has done to the waystation's old infrastructure.

Why three clocks

Phase 9 is an evidence-gathering operation in active opposition. A party that hides perfectly (Alert low) but lets cleaners work (Record Destruction high) leaves with no evidence. A party that engages cleaners loudly (Alert high) but preserves records (Record Destruction low) leaves with everything they came for but in active pursuit. A party that uses magic or force aggressively (Breath and Stone high) compromises the site itself — possibly fatally for everyone inside.

Pacing the clocks

Three clocks is intentionally more than Phase 8's two. The phase is shorter (1–2 sessions vs Phase 8's 2–3), the chambers are tighter, and the consequences should feel layered. The GM should track all three openly — the party should feel pressure on three axes at once.

Starting values

Phase 9 starting values depend on the opening branch from Phase 8. See the Arrival journal for branch-specific starting values. Default (Opening A — Clean Entry):

  • Under-Road Alert Clock: 0.
  • Record Destruction Clock: 1.
  • Breath and Stone Clock: 0.

Holding the clocks

Careful play, reading marks, bracing supports, controlling light, capturing or neutralising burn agents, and securing rooms can pause or reduce the clocks. Specifically:

  • Disabling the route bell at the Warden Post (Sleight of Hand DC 15) prevents one Alert advance.
  • Securing the Burn Furnace (subduing or distracting the cleaner there) prevents two Record Destruction advances.
  • Bracing a damaged support with mason's tools (DC 13) prevents one Breath and Stone advance.
  • Speaking to Savax in route language (Persuasion DC 13 just to engage) holds Alert at its current value during the conversation.

Under-Road Alert Clock (0–6)

Tracks how aware Witness Station Seven becomes of the party as an active problem rather than a procedural anomaly.

What advances this clock

TriggerAdvance
Phase 8 ended with Forced Entry (Method E at the Permission Door)Start at +2
Phase 8 ended with Lockdown 4+ (Opening C: Enemy Ahead)Start at +3
Phase 8 ended with structural damage (Opening E)Start at +4
Phase 8 was refused, party returns later (Opening F)Start at +1
Failed Stealth in the Arrival Stair or eighth landing+1
Loud magic (audible cantrips, area spells, illumination effects)+1 per occurrence
Visible light in dark passages (uncovered torch, daylight spell)+1
Combat in the Account Hall+2 (initial), +1 per round after the first
Prisoners shouting or panicking+1
Triggering the route bell at the Warden Post+2
Forcing the Black Ledger Gate+2
Letting a route-cleaner flee with information+2
Tally Sentinel waking+1

What holds this clock

  • Speaking to Savax or other wardens in route language (the GM may rule a successful conversation holds advance for that scene).
  • Disabling the signal cord at the Warden Post.
  • Subduing cleaners without killing them and without letting them flee.
  • Moving with stealth (passive Stealth 14+ for the party).

Effects by segment

Alert 0–1: Routine

The waystation is operating normally. Cleaners are working at their tasks but not actively watching for intruders. Savax is at the Warden Post but reading paperwork, not the slit. The party can move freely if they are not making noise.

Alert 2: Inner shutters close

Lamps in the Account Hall are dimmed to half. Cleaners are told to be more deliberate. Savax begins glancing through the slit window every few minutes. Stealth rolls in the Account Hall are at disadvantage.

Alert 3: Route marks covered

Some route marks in the Marker Gallery are draped with cloth to obscure them from observation. Cleaners are deliberately blocking visual access to the Witness Registry Wall. Savax has dispatched a runner to the Black Ledger Gate to prepare for a possible breach. Reading registry entries requires removing cloth (advances Record Destruction +1).

Alert 4: Warden team mobilises

Savax leaves the Warden Post and moves to the Account Hall to address the situation directly. Two cleaners are armed and positioned at the gallery entrance. A Silent Courier is dispatched through the Black Ledger Gate to the next waystation with an emergency message. This is the trigger for the Correction encounter (next page).

Alert 5: Lower gate seals

The Black Ledger Gate's mechanism is set to refuse all routine declarations. Methods A–E now require additional checks at +2 DC each. Only Methods F (Force) and G (Bypass) remain at their original DCs. The gate is no longer in standard operating mode; it is in defensive mode.

Alert 6: Deep courier departs

The Silent Courier dispatched at Alert 4 has reached the next waystation. The deeper authority's mark has been read by a courier with higher rank, and a deep courier departs that next station upward, headed for Witness Station Seven. The party has 2–4 hours of in-fiction time before the deep courier arrives. Whoever it is, it is not someone the party wants to meet here. Phase 9 must conclude within this window, or Phase 10 begins under critical pursuit.

Record Destruction Clock (0–6)

Tracks how much evidence has been burned, scraped, moved, or corrupted by the route's own personnel ahead of the party's investigation. Higher values mean less evidence available to take to surface.

What advances this clock

TriggerAdvance
Phase 8 Opening C (Enemy Ahead) at startStart at +3
Phase 8 Opening F (Returned After Refusing) at startStart at +4
Each short rest (1 hour) taken inside the waystation while cleaners are active+1
Each long rest taken inside the waystation+3
Failing to secure the Burn Furnace (letting a cleaner work there)+1 per round of cleaner activity
Questioning captives without securing the rooms they came from+1 per scene
Making a deal with Savax that includes “allowing record processing to continue”+1
Forcing the Black Ledger Gate (route panics and burns at the next station)+1 (after passage)
Failing a Perception check at the Burn Furnace (cleaner gets one item into the fire)+1
Failing a Stealth check while route-cleaners are conducting a sweep+1

What holds or reduces this clock

  • Securing the Burn Furnace (subduing/distracting the cleaner there): pauses advance.
  • Securing the Old Route Office (preserving the half-finished Conversion Note): pauses advance for that evidence.
  • Securing the Witness Registry Wall (preventing scraping): pauses advance.
  • Capturing route-cleaners alive and binding them: holds clock at current value.
  • Pretending to be route personnel and ordering cleaners to stop their sweep: Deception DC 16; success reduces clock by 1.

Effects by segment

Records 0–1: Routine processing

The route's standard maintenance — one cleaner at the Burn Furnace working through routine items. No urgency. All major evidence is still recoverable.

Records 2: Surface-name slips burning

Cleaners are now burning surface name slips in the Burn Furnace. The H.V.A. (Andren) slip is in the queue. Recovery requires the party to reach the Burn Furnace before the slip is fed. Approximately 10 in-fiction minutes from this point until the slip is gone.

Records 3: Witness tokens removed

The Toll-Token Cage has been visited and several witness tokens removed. The party will find fewer tokens than expected. The Warden Post's token box may be empty by the time they reach it. The Witness Method (A) at the Black Ledger Gate may be harder — the party needs to scavenge or substitute.

Records 4: H.V.A. line scraped

The Andren entry on the Witness Registry Wall has been scraped clean. The wax plate marked “Andren” is gone — either taken to the Burn Furnace or pocketed by a cleaner for offsite disposal. The party may still find evidence of Haskur's transit elsewhere (Old Route Office tablet, Warden Instruction Strip), but the most direct evidence on the wall is lost.

Records 5: Blooming Rot crates moved

The Supply Cache has been opened and the most incriminating crates moved through the Black Ledger Gate toward the next waystation. The party will find only routine supplies and a few sample packets. The Recovery Crate Tag (Handout 4) is unavailable; the party must recover this evidence from circumstantial cues alone.

Records 6: Black Ledger copied and destroyed

The cleaners have completed an emergency copy of the most important registry entries for transfer below, and have begun scraping or pulverising the original registry wall in the Account Hall. Most evidence is lost. The party can still leave with some pieces — the Witness Tally Fragment (if they took it earlier), the Conversion Note, possibly the Furnace Scrap if they were quick — but the larger picture is broken.

The race condition

Record Destruction is the clock the party should feel most urgent about. Every minute they spend deliberating, the cleaners are working. The GM should describe this in-fiction: “You hear the chisel on stone again. Whatever they were scraping a minute ago, they are scraping more of now.”

Breath and Stone Clock (0–6)

Tracks environmental danger — air thinning, water pressure rising, supports groaning, lamps burning wrong, and the structural condition of the waystation overall.

What advances this clock

TriggerAdvance
Phase 8 Structural Failure Clock 4+ at descentStart at +2
Phase 8 Structural Failure Clock 5+ at descent (Opening E)Start at +3
Loud explosions, area damage, evocation spells in confined chambers+1 per occurrence
Uncontrolled fire (lamp tipped, oil ignited, fire spell escaping)+1, possibly +2 if extensive
Breaking old seals (forcing the Black Ledger Gate, opening sealed chambers with force)+2
Damaging cistern vents or grates+1
Collapsing route walls (Force at the gate, mason work)+1 per major collapse
Heavy combat near supports (Account Hall pillars, gallery archways)+1 per round of heavy combat
Opening the wrong sluice valve in the Cistern Slit access+2 (and triggers a water pulse hazard)
Ignoring environmental warnings (lamps burning sideways, supports groaning)+1 per ignored warning

What holds or reduces this clock

  • Bracing damaged supports with mason's tools (DC 13): pauses advance.
  • Smothering accidental fires within 1 round: prevents +1.
  • Closing properly-fitted sluice valves: pauses advance.
  • Heeding environmental warnings (PCs slow down, take time, retreat from unstable areas): pauses advance.
  • Repairing a vent or grate that was damaged: requires the right tool and time, but reduces the clock by 1.

Effects by segment

Breath and Stone 0–1: Old but stable

The waystation's 5th-century mason work is holding. The air is breathable; the cistern is flowing normally; the supports are in their expected positions. No unusual sounds.

Breath and Stone 2: Air thins

The party feels the air change. Candles gutter inward. Breathing becomes slightly harder; sound feels muffled. PCs making sustained exertion (combat past round 3, climbing, running) make Constitution save DC 13 or take 1 level of fatigue (operational penalty: disadvantage on the next two ability checks). Mitigation: stop, rest in the chamber with the best airflow (typically near the Cistern Slit), open vents.

Breath and Stone 3: Cistern pressure rises

The cistern slit begins to pulse with intermittent pressure surges. Anyone in or beside the slit makes Dexterity save DC 14 or be knocked prone and pushed 10 feet downstream. Lamps near the slit flicker irregularly — the air movement is no longer reliable for them.

Breath and Stone 4: Old supports groan

The waystation's wooden support timbers (where they exist, mostly in the gallery and the Sleeping Alcoves corridor) begin to creak audibly under stress. Mason's tools DC 13 identifies which supports are at risk. Anyone running through a chamber when a support fails: Dexterity save DC 13 or 2d6 bludgeoning damage from falling rock.

Breath and Stone 5: Lower gallery floods or collapses

One of the side galleries fails. The GM chooses which:

  • Sleeping Alcoves corridor — partly collapses. Movement through is now Athletics DC 14 to climb over rubble. Some niches are buried.
  • Marker Gallery — one section of wall comes down, destroying a portion of the route marks. Evidence is lost.
  • Cistern Slit (east end) — the maintenance bridges collapse, making Method G (Bypass) much harder; Athletics DC 16 and at risk of being swept downstream.

Breath and Stone 6: Retreat route dangerous

The upper stair (back toward Tarnsmere) is now critically unstable. Climbing it up requires Athletics DC 16, with Dexterity save DC 14 or take 3d6 bludgeoning damage from falling stone on a failure. Some sections of the stair are impassable; the party must find an alternate route (the Cistern Slit, or a new path the GM seeds for Phase 10).

Cascading failures

If both Alert and Breath and Stone reach 6 simultaneously, the waystation is in unrecoverable failure. The route's deep courier (Alert 6) is coming up while the upper stair is collapsing (Breath and Stone 6). The party has 15–30 in-fiction minutes to either commit to going deeper (through the Black Ledger Gate, foreclosing return) or attempting evacuation under fire. This is the highest-stakes outcome.

pages.name: Encounter — The Correction at the Account Hall

text.content:
Encounter — "The Correction at the Account Hall"

The major (optional) encounter of Phase 9. Run only when triggered; do not force it.

Triggers

  • Party enters the Account Hall loudly while route personnel are present.
  • Party interferes with the Burn Furnace and is detected.
  • Party attempts to open the Black Ledger Gate by Force while wardens are alive.
  • Party brings a captured Kethren or Haskur openly into the Account Hall.
  • Under-Road Alert Clock reaches 4.

Enemy lineup

  • Route Warden Savax (CR 5). Tier 2 mage-lite leader; see his actor entry. Fights from the Warden Post slit or from the Account Hall's central counter.
  • 2 Route-Cleaners (CR 1 each). Spy archetype with burn oil and chisel-knives. Prioritise destroying evidence over killing.
  • 1 Silent Courier (CR 3). Scout/monk hybrid; see actor entry. Tries to escape with the “second page” or a lower-route token rather than fight.
  • Optional: 1 Tally Sentinel (CR 4). Awake only if the party Force-attacked the Permission Door in Phase 8 or has already forced the Black Ledger Gate. If present, it stands in the gallery entrance, observing.

Total CR weighting: approximately CR 9–11 against a party of 4–6 PCs at levels 8–11. Tunable; reduce by removing the sentinel or one cleaner if the party is lower-tier.

Terrain features

  • Stone counters — half-cover when adjacent. Provide a +2 AC bonus from ranged attacks.
  • Token slots — PCs can place tokens during combat as interaction actions. If a PC places a witness token in Bay 2 mid-combat, the gate's mechanism upgrades the party's procedural standing — potentially de-escalating the encounter if Savax chooses to recognise the declaration.
  • Low light — disadvantage on Perception past 30 feet for PCs without darkvision.
  • Burn oil reservoirs — the lamp brackets at the corners. Shooting or shattering one (AC 12 for the brass reservoir) spills oil; an open flame ignites it for 2d6 fire damage in a 5-foot square per round for 3 rounds.
  • Hanging tally plates — some plates hang from iron hooks at chest height. They can be pulled down for cover or to obscure a registry column being scraped (and to advance Record Destruction by 0 instead of 1 if a cleaner was working on that column).
  • Shutter slits — Savax can fire arrows or speak through the Warden Post slit while remaining behind cover. Visibility from the slit to the hall floor is excellent; from the floor up to the slit is limited.
  • Side ducts to the cistern slit — about 3 feet wide, present along the south wall. PCs can dive into them as a bonus action with a Dexterity DC 13 check.

Enemy tactical objectives

The enemies have specific objectives that take priority over killing the party:

  1. The two route-cleaners: burn the H.V.A. record and the Recovery Crate Tag. They will go through the party to reach the Burn Furnace if needed, but they are not trying to kill anyone — they are trying to complete their burn list.
  2. The Silent Courier: escape with the “second page” (a small leather case at her belt containing a sealed dispatch for the next waystation) and a lower-route token. She will fight only to clear an escape route. She will jump into the cistern slit if cornered.
  3. Savax: classify or delay the party. His goal is to get the party to declare a category — even falsely — which lets him process them rather than kill them. He will offer dialogue throughout combat. The party can “win” by talking with him.
  4. The Tally Sentinel (if present): witness and record. It will only attack if attacked, and it will not pursue past the Lower Route Fork.

Win conditions for the party

The party wins if they achieve any one:

  • Preserve the H.V.A. record and the Recovery Crate Tag (drives the cleaners away from the Burn Furnace by force, intimidation, or trick).
  • Capture the Silent Courier alive (huge Phase 10 intelligence prize).
  • Open the Lower Gate (Black Ledger Gate) without forcing it.
  • Force the route personnel to retreat through the gate, leaving the waystation in the party's hands for now.

Noncombat resolutions

Savax is the only enemy who will engage in dialogue mid-combat. The party can:

  • Present a valid token for a category — Savax stops fighting and converses.
  • Declare a witnessed transfer in route language — even falsely, if plausible.
  • Bargain with Savax — offer him something he wants (a clean record, no surface exposure, etc.).
  • Expose that Korre channel is compromised — this is news; he may negotiate to learn more.
  • Use Kethren's authority if Kethren is present and cooperating.
  • Threaten to damage the station — works if the Breath and Stone Clock is already 3+, where the threat is credible.
  • Offer Haskur as unreliable recovered cargo — Savax knows Haskur's classification and may accept Haskur in exchange for letting the party pass.
  • Trick the warden into correcting a false record — clever and risky, but possible: the party convinces Savax that his own records contain an error, and that they're here to correct it on behalf of the deeper authority. Requires Deception DC 18 or persistent successful Insight reads.

Morale

Route personnel do not fight to the death. They retreat if:

  • Half their number are down (incapacitated or dead).
  • Their tactical objective is foreclosed (e.g., the Burn Furnace is secured).
  • A reasonable bargain is offered.
  • The Tally Sentinel falls (if present — the sentinel's defeat signals to them that the route's procedural authority has failed here).

They value records over bodies: a cleaner will accept death to feed one more item to the fire. They value route continuity over revenge: a cleaner who escapes will not return for vengeance, only to inform the next waystation.

Enemy Presence Sliders

Enemy presence at Witness Station Seven scales with the Alert Clock. The table below summarises starting presence at each Alert level. Adjust to taste for party composition and HP/resource state.

Presence by Alert level

AlertPersonnel present
0–1Savax (Warden Post). 1 Route-Cleaner (Burn Furnace, routine work). 1 Under-Road Scout (eighth landing, not yet observing). 0 Silent Couriers in transit.
2Savax. 2 Route-Cleaners (1 Burn Furnace, 1 Registry Wall). 1 Under-Road Scout. 1 Silent Courier passing through (in the Marker Gallery).
3Savax. 3 Route-Cleaners (Burn Furnace, Registry, Toll-Token Cage). 1 Under-Road Scout. 1 Silent Courier in transit toward Black Ledger Gate.
4Savax (Account Hall now). 3 Route-Cleaners (all actively defending evidence). 1 Under-Road Scout. 1 Silent Courier dispatched outward. Tally Sentinel: dormant unless previously woken.
5Savax. 4 Route-Cleaners (one armed, one armed-and-on-burn-duty, two as defenders). 1 Scout. 1 Silent Courier outbound + 1 inbound. Tally Sentinel: activates.
6Savax (last stand at Account Hall or attempted escape). 5 Route-Cleaners (full defensive posture; some have abandoned destruction and are fighting). 1 Scout. Deep Courier inbound from next station (arrives 2–4 hours). Tally Sentinel active. Reinforcements imminent.

Enemy types in summary

Full stat block guidance is in the Phase 9 NPC journal. Quick reference:

A. Route Warden Savax

Procedural controller. CR 5. Uses command, positioning, gate control, and knowledge. Not eager to die. Negotiable.

B. Route-Cleaner (any)

Erases records, burns paper, defaces marks. CR 1. Uses smoke, knives, chisels, burn oil. Prioritises evidence destruction over combat. Re-uses the Route-Cleanser Agent statblock from Phase 7.

C. Silent Courier

Fast, precise, narrow-space movement. CR 3. Carries packets, tokens, or sealed dispatches. Avoids combat; runs through cistern slit if cornered.

D. Under-Road Scout

Watches, signals, flees. CR 1/2. Excellent in darkness. Re-uses the Silent Scout statblock from Phase 7.

E. Tally Sentinel (construct)

Witness construct. CR 4. Activates on forced gate use or major procedural violations. Bypassable by procedure.

Returning enemies from Phase 7 and 8

The party may recognise Route-Cleaners (Phase 7) and the Silent Scout pattern (Phase 7). They are the same broad type. The route is not staffed by an army; it is staffed by the same kind of people who work above, in the same operational style, with different paperwork. This continuity is intentional.

Enemy priorities (overall)

  1. Preserve lower-route secrecy.
  2. Destroy surface-linked records before they leave the station.
  3. Prevent unauthorised witnesses from passing the Black Ledger Gate.
  4. Move remaining packets onward.
  5. Extract or silence Haskur if he is a liability (only if he is present in the cell or with the party).
  6. Recover Kethren if he is captured (if Kethren is alive and with the party, route personnel try to take him from them).
  7. Avoid revealing higher authority. They will not speak about “E.”, the deeper authority mark, or what lies beyond the next waystation. They will die rather than disclose this.

Environmental Hazards

Six hazards. Each has a clear sign, a save mechanic, and mitigation. None of them are intended to kill PCs outright; they are intended to make the party respect the waystation as a place that does not want them.

A. Thin Air

  • Signs: candles gutter inward, breathing grows shallow, sound feels muffled. Lamps burn yellow-white instead of yellow.
  • Trigger: Breath and Stone Clock 2+.
  • Mechanic: Constitution save DC 13 after exertion (sustained combat past round 3, climbing, running). Failure: one level of fatigue (operational: disadvantage on the next two ability checks; persists until rest in better air).
  • Mitigation: rest in chambers with cistern airflow (Account Hall south side, Sleeping Alcoves), open additional vents (Investigation DC 13 to find a venting plate), or slow movement.

B. Cistern Pulse

  • Signs: the water level in the cistern slit visibly rises and falls. A distant hum from the eastern grate.
  • Trigger: Breath and Stone Clock 3+. Fires automatically when a PC enters the slit at this clock level.
  • Mechanic: Dexterity save DC 14 or knocked prone and pushed 10 feet downstream. PCs on bridges above: DC 12 or prone.
  • Mitigation: Careful observation (Perception DC 13 timing the pulse) grants advantage. Waiting for a low-pulse window (Survival DC 14, requires 5 minutes of observation) avoids the hazard.

C. Burn Furnace Backdraft

  • Signs: the brazier glows brighter than expected; the iron tongs are warm to the touch.
  • Trigger: opening the furnace door wide during active feeding, or using fire magic in the chamber.
  • Mechanic: Dexterity save DC 14 or take 2d6 fire damage; half on save.
  • Mitigation: Investigation DC 13 predicts the risk. Open the door only narrowly (no save needed but harder to extract items: Sleight of Hand DC 14).

D. Tally Plate Collapse

  • Signs: a slight visible tilt to one of the hanging tally plates; iron hooks creak when the plate is touched.
  • Trigger: Combat or heavy movement near a plate; or Breath and Stone Clock 4+.
  • Mechanic: Dexterity save DC 13 or 2d6 bludgeoning damage from a falling plate.
  • Mitigation: Mason's Tools or Perception DC 13 spots instability. PCs can secure a plate (Athletics DC 12, 1 round) to prevent fall.

E. Route Bell

  • Signs: the bell hangs visibly at the Warden Post. The signal cord runs to the Black Ledger Gate.
  • Trigger: deliberately struck by a warden, or accidentally struck (the cord can be pulled inadvertently in combat in the Warden Post).
  • Mechanic: Not damaging. Advances the Under-Road Alert Clock by +1 or +2 depending on cadence (warden uses controlled cadence; accidental strikes are erratic and trigger only +1).
  • Mitigation: Disable the bell (Thieves' Tools DC 15 or clever physical sabotage — muffling with cloth, removing the striker), or disable the signal cord (Sleight of Hand DC 15).

F. Black Ledger Gate Backlash

  • Signs: the gate emits a low harmonic when wrong tokens or marks are placed.
  • Trigger: Forced opening, catastrophic failure of any method, or use of the counterfeit token from the Toll-Token Cage.
  • Mechanic: All creatures within 15 feet make Constitution save DC 14 or take 4d8 thunder damage; half on save (less for serious failures: 2d8 instead of 4d8).
  • Mitigation: Don't force the gate. Identify the counterfeit token before using it (Investigation DC 14). Advances the Breath and Stone Clock; not permanently blocking but uncomfortable.

How to play hazards at the table

  • Telegraph each hazard before it fires. Describe the sign. Let PCs make Perception checks. Let the warning matter.
  • Reward mitigation immediately. If a PC closes a vent, reduce the relevant clock. If a PC predicts a hazard, give advantage on the save.
  • Use hazards sparingly. Phase 9 has six potential hazards but should fire two or three across the whole phase. They are flavour and pressure, not damage taxation.

Faction Reactions to Phase 9 Events

Phase 9 events ripple back to surface factions only when the party returns to surface (Outcome G), or when later phases create indirect contact (a captured route personnel testifying, a piece of recovered paperwork being formally produced in surface court, etc.). The factions react as follows.

Caelith Dunivar

  • Cannot communicate with the party once they have descended below the Permission Door. The route does not carry surface messages.
  • Will care intensely about every piece of evidence recovered. The Furnace Scrap (Handout 5) will be the single most important document Caelith has seen since the original Phase 1 evidence.
  • If the party returns with the Witness Tally Fragment, the Recovery Crate Tag, and the Furnace Scrap together, Caelith immediately understands the conspiracy's recovery logic. He will reorganise his entire surface strategy on the strength of that understanding.
  • If the party does not return, Caelith will eventually mount a recovery expedition of his own — not in Phase 9, but as a Phase 11 or 12 thread the GM can seed.
  • Caelith's standing on the Yeomanry Council: by Phase 9, Caelith's institutional standing is contested (per Phase 5 events). Phase 9 evidence, brought back, may restore him; or it may end his career, depending on how the Council reads it. The party's return choice (Outcome G: full retreat) makes this politically real.

Greyhawk authorities

  • The Watch (Iren Velash and her allies) are not relevant to the waystation itself. They have no jurisdiction below.
  • If the party returns with Phase 9 evidence and Iren is alive, she will help the party hide it — but she will be uncomfortable about the lack of established legal framework for what they're showing her. A registry that does not contain names is not, in surface terms, evidence of anything.
  • If the party returns with a captured route personnel — the Silent Courier, Savax, or a route-cleaner — Iren can quietly hold them under a non-public arrest while the party works out what to do. She will not press charges that would require disclosing the under-road; she will hold them as “persons of investigative interest” for as long as legally possible.
  • If the party caused public violence above earlier (Phase 6 brawls, Phase 7 confrontations, Phase 8 forced entry), Greyhawk authorities may complicate Phase 10. The Watch can only protect the party so long.

Guild of Miners and Jewelers

  • The Guild will deny that the under-road exists if directly asked. This is the institutional response.
  • However, individual senior Guild members — some of whom the party met in Phase 7 (Dalmor Vetch) — may privately confirm that the Guild has long known certain “permitted descents” were not what they were filed as. Vetch could be a Phase 10 ally if the party brings him Phase 9 evidence.
  • The Guild's old erased claims (mentioned in Phase 7) may become Phase 10 evidence if the party returns with proof. The Guild has motive to suppress; the party has motive to expose. This is a Phase 10–12 storyline.

Cindren & Vhal residual network

  • The C&V residuals are a surface-facing financial node. They do not control the waystation. They may have fed assets, claims, and bonded paper into it over years.
  • The Recovery Crate Tag (Handout 4) connects bonded claims to crop damage in a way that, if surfaced publicly, could trigger civil and criminal proceedings against the C&V residual leadership.
  • The C&V residuals are unaware of Phase 9 activity at the waystation. They will learn only through the deeper authority's reporting chain, which is slow and irregular. They have several weeks of grace before they realise their surface paperwork is exposed.

V. Korre's channel

  • Korre's surface authority ends at or before the Final Surface Landing. The waystation's records show her channel as closed.
  • If Korre is alive and free in Phase 9 (she escaped Phase 7 or was never captured), she is now operating under reduced authority. She knows her channel is closed. She may be desperate.
  • If Korre is captured or dead, the channel is permanently closed from the surface side as well. Her surface successor (the GM can name one as needed for Phase 10) has not been authorised by the deeper authority and is operating informally.

The deeper authority

  • Not named. The party should leave Phase 9 knowing it exists, knowing its mark, knowing it operates above Korre, Sereth, and the C&V residuals — and not knowing who it is.
  • The deeper authority does not care about the party as individuals. It cares about the route's procedural integrity. If the party leaves the waystation having damaged the route, the deeper authority will respond — but slowly, through procedural channels, and not in person. This is one of the campaign's signature horror elements: there is no boss fight. There is paperwork.
  • If the party makes it through Phase 9 cleanly (Outcome A: Quiet Witness), the deeper authority may not even know they were there until a routine audit weeks later.

Brane / Closure faction (Loftwick)

  • Not present at the waystation.
  • If the party returns with evidence, the Closure faction will claim the party overstepped Yeomanry Council authority by descending. This is a Phase 11 political fight, not a Phase 9 problem.
  • Phase 9 should not try to resolve the Yeomanry's surface politics. Hold those threads for the surface-return phases.

The PCs' personal threads

Per-PC threads (see Phase 8's per-PC integration) continue in Phase 9 but with light touch. Phase 9 is not the right phase for big personal-thread payoffs — the party is in a stranger's house, learning to read the walls. Save heavy thread development for Phase 10 onwards.

Light touches that work in Phase 9:

  • Kitty: her chthonic recognition continues. The waystation's stone “reads” her more easily than the surface stone did. If Kitty places her palm on the registry wall, the GM can note privately that one mark in the column near her hand warms faintly.
  • Cam: the registry wall holds entries that, cross-referenced, identify a few halfling cousins from Hesp Olfair's list as having been moved through this waystation. Cam can confirm what happened to one or two more of his missing.
  • Selvara: her dream-direction continues. Selvara's player can note that the left-hand corridor (Recovery Route) feels “wrong” in her dream sense; the centre (Escort Route) feels “known”; the right (Supply Route) feels “hidden.” This is GM-noted only, not mechanical.
  • Alicia: her pact-mark continues cold. No relief yet.
  • Elle: if she used her iron key in Phase 8, the order's awareness is now formal. She may have received a brief letter from her abbess (delivered via Aerdy commercial rapid courier to the Tarnsmere site, recovered with Caelith's pre-descent note packet) acknowledging the order's old paperwork — but no instruction yet. The order is waiting to see what Elle does.
  • Gianni: her geographic-erasure thread now intersects with the route. The waystation's existence proves her hypothesis — missing roads, missing surveyors, all of it. Gianni is the PC who would understand the conspiracy's logic most quickly in Phase 9.
